title: NIS2 Human Resources Security Measures Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(i) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) (Human resources and access control) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.30: NIS2 Human Resources Security Measures Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.30' running on index=training (role, completion_date), OktaIM2:log (deprovision events), nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:jml_events` stores `user_id`, `completion_date`, `course_code`, `employment_status` from joiner-mover-leaver feeds (often via HTTP Event Collector). `index=auth` includes `sourcetype=OktaIM2:log` lifecycle lines with `eventType`, `target.user.id`, and `published` for deprovisions. Privileged coverage uses lookup `nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv` columns `role_name`, `user_id`, `manager`. Optional assurance joins `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` via Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud (Splunkbase 6553) supplies normalized auth telemetry.
app: Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud (Splunkbase 6553), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HTTP Event Collector (HEC for HR JSON or CSV drops)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv
| join user_id type=left [
    search index=hr sourcetype=hr:jml_events earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_training by user_id
]
| eval training_age=if(isnotnull(last_training), round((now()-strptime(last_training,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0), null())
| where training_age > 365 OR isnull(last_training)
| table role_name, user_id, last_training, training_age
description: Surfaces privileged-role people who lack a recent security-training completion in the HR feed, turning policy language about workforce safeguards into a measurable backlog with owner and role context.
value: Reduces last-minute audit panic by giving People and Security teams a shared, dated list of overdue training tied to high-privilege accounts—so fixes happen during the year, not the night before a regulator visit.
implementation: (1) Land `hr:jml_events` after HRIS approval; scrub PII per privacy review. (2) Confirm Okta TA (6553) runs on a Heavy Forwarder with API rate limits set to **1200**/minute. (3) Save report `UC-22.2.30` weekly; optional Slack digest. (4) Pair deprovision latency checks in a sibling panel querying `index=auth`. (5) Document exceptions in `nis2_hr_training_exceptions.csv` with `expires_on`.
visualization: Table of overdue training, single value % compliant, bar chart by role family, optional timeline of completion waves.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud

knownFalsePositives:
• **HR file arrived late after annual campaign** — Distinguish: worker finished classroom training but LMS ingest lags **48 hours**. Suppress: time-bound exception rows in `nis2_hr_training_exceptions.csv` with `reason=ingest_delay`.
• **Contractors on different curriculum** — Distinguish: `course_code` not mapped to NIS2 requirement. Suppress: lookup `nis2_training_curriculum_map.csv` excludes those `course_code` values via `where !match(course_code,"NONSCOPE")` after joining course data.
• **Shared break-glass accounts** — Distinguish: `user_id` belongs to vault-managed identity without training expectation. Suppress: mark `training_exempt=true` in roster CSV.
• **Duplicate HR events** — Distinguish: replays cause `completion_date` flapping. Suppress: `dedup user_id sortby - _time` in the subsearch before `stats`.
• **Parental leave** — Distinguish: HR status `on_leave` with policy waiver. Suppress: join `hr:jml_events` for `employment_status` and `where employment_status!="on_leave"` or explicit waiver lookup.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• HR and Legal sign off on storing `user_id` hashes versus clear text; document DPIA reference.
• HEC token on HF restricted to `index=hr`, sourcetype forced with `props.conf` `WRITE_META = true` optional for indexer fields.
• Splunk Add-on for Okta (Splunkbase 6553) with service account + OAuth secret stored under **Settings > Account passwords**.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) optional for policy evidence with polling interval **300 seconds**.
• RBAC role `hr_security_read` limited to HR indexes and lookup directory.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Build a lightweight transform that maps HR CSV columns to `user_id`, `completion_date`, `course_code`. For Okta, enable lifecycle inputs per vendor README; confirm `eventType` for `user.lifecycle.deactivate` appears within **10 minutes** of HR termination in ServiceNow.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save SPL as `UC-22.2.30`. Schedule Monday 08:00 local. Use `where len(user_id)>0` guard. For executive privacy, mask `user_id` display using `anonymize` command in presentation-layer dashboards only.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv
| join user_id type=left [
    search index=hr sourcetype=hr:jml_events earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_training by user_id
]
```

Understanding this SPL: `training_age` null triggers catch people who never appears in HR feed—validate against onboarding dates to avoid false escalations.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick five users; compare `last_training` to LMS UI. Export screenshot packet for internal audit. Spot-check `index=auth` for deprovision delay: `search index=auth sourcetype=OktaIM2:log user=<id> earliest=-7d`.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Add HR ServiceNow link in drilldown. If join misses, ensure HR feed lowercases IDs while roster uses mixed case—normalize with `| eval user_id=lower(user_id)` on both sides.

Step 5 — Governance
Quarterly attestation: CHRO signs roster accuracy. Archive Splunk CSV with `evidenceSigning`. Retention: align `index=hr` to employee records policy—commonly **7 years**.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a fresh list of people with powerful computer access who still owe their yearly safety-and-security lessons, so the right managers can nudge them before it becomes a compliance surprise.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel/competent-authority review.
evidence: Weekly `UC-22.2.30` export listing `user_id`, `role_name`, `training_age`, attached HR acknowledgement tickets, archived in `audit_evidence` with `nis2_clause=Art.21(2)(i)`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Add `user_id=privileged_tester` to the roster without any `hr:jml_events` rows; running the search must return that user with blank `last_training` and null `training_age` flagged by the `isnull(last_training)` clause.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Insert a compliant `hr:jml_events` event with `completion_date` within 180 days for the same tester; rerun and confirm the user disappears from the overdue table.
